title: palaquium lanceolatum
intro: Palaquium lanceolatum, also known as the lanceolate palaquium or lanceolate stinkwood, is a species of plant in the family Sapotaceae, native to Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat wounds, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Palaquium lanceolatum include its potential to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as a treatment for skin conditions and digestive problems. Palaquium lanceolatum is often prepared as a herbal tea or infused in oil to be used topically, taking advantage of its medicinal properties.
